title: Swiss Army Man
logline: A suicidal man stranded on a deserted island befriends a flatulent corpse that comes to life, and together they journey through the wilderness toward home while building an elaborate fantasy world to help the corpse remember what it means to be human.
antagonistOrConflict: Isolation, shame, and the impossibility of authentic human connection
tone: Absurdist, tender, melancholic, whimsical, and deeply earnest beneath layers of grotesque humor
themes: Loneliness and the human need for connection
Shame and social conditioning around bodily functions and vulnerability
The construction of identity and memory
The courage required to be authentically oneself
Death and what makes life worth living

actBreaks.description: Manny speaks his first words and the journey transforms from pure survival into an emotional quest to help Manny remember life and love.

validation.ruling: Scene #28 marks an irreversible shift when Hank, frustrated and starving, admits 'all of this, you and I, right now. Probably just in my imagination' yet chooses to continue anyway—sitting beside Manny to teach him about desire through the magazine, fundamentally committing to the relationship and fantasy-building that will define Act 2, unable to return to mere survival mode.

actBreaks.description: After the near-drowning and Manny's existential crisis about whether life is worth living, they commit fully to each other and the fantasy collapses into confrontation with reality.

validation.ruling: Scene #80 is a brief dream flash, not a structural break. Scene #71 (p63, EXT. FOREST #10 CAMPFIRE - NIGHT) is the true Act 2 break where Hank and Manny's fantasy world catastrophically collapses—the bear attack shatters their constructed reality, Manny discovers Sarah wasn't his, and their journey reaches a crisis point that forces them into Act 3's reckoning with truth versus illusion.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a circular journey that begins and ends at the ocean's edge, but with Hank fundamentally transformed. The structure mirrors the psychological arc: isolation → connection through fantasy → confrontation with reality → acceptance. The middle section (scenes 25-62) is dominated by an extended montage sequence where Hank builds elaborate sets to teach Manny about life, creating a nested reality that becomes increasingly complex. This isn't traditional three-act structure so much as a spiral: each layer of fantasy Hank constructs brings him closer to confronting his own shame and loneliness. The script's use of dream sequences, flashbacks, and constructed realities creates a non-linear emotional experience even as the physical journey moves forward. The final act doesn't resolve so much as it exposes—peeling back the fantasy to reveal the raw truth underneath, then offering a moment of transcendent absurdity as Manny farts one last time, suggesting that authenticity and connection are possible even in our most embarrassing, vulnerable moments.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately uneven, mirroring Hank's psychological state. The opening is slow and contemplative (scenes 1-20), building dread and isolation. The pace accelerates dramatically once Manny speaks (scene 24), then explodes into the montage sequences (scenes 25-62) where time compresses and fantasy takes over. The script then oscillates between intimate two-hander dialogue scenes and hallucinatory dream sequences (scenes 71-109), creating a fever-dream quality as Manny's consciousness fragments. The final movement (scenes 110-128) rushes toward exposure and revelation, with very short scenes cutting rapidly between different perspectives as the real world intrudes on their private universe.

incitingIncident.description: Hank rides the farting corpse like a jet ski across the ocean by singing and pulling on the man's tie, escaping the island in a moment of rapturous freedom.

validation.ruling: Scene #3 shatters Hank's suicidal status quo when he successfully rides the farting corpse across the ocean like a jet ski, experiencing 'pure freedom' and rapture that overtakes logic—this impossible event forces him into a new reality where the corpse has magical properties, establishing the central relationship and irreversible stakes that drive the entire narrative.

midpoint.description: Drunk on the rooftop, Manny asks Sarah/Hank to make a ring with his fingers and proposes marriage, but Hank pulls back from their near-kiss, citing his parents' unhappy marriage.

validation.ruling: Scene #53 marks Hank's shift from reactive fantasy-building to proactive emotional honesty when Manny proposes marriage and Hank, drunk and vulnerable, reveals his deepest fear about his parents' failed marriage and his own unworthiness—this confession represents a point of no return where Hank can no longer hide behind the Sarah fantasy, raising personal stakes and forcing him toward truth.

climax.description: Hank drags Manny onto a sand bar and tries to shove him into the ocean but he washes back; Hank cradles Manny looking for signs of life while Sarah stands twenty feet away watching, a police officer behind her, and Hank's Dad on the rocks; Hank apologizes to Manny for screwing him up and says he wanted to give him all the things Hank thought he didn't deserve, then turns to see Sarah approaching.

validation.ruling: Scene #128 delivers peak confrontation when Hank cradles the lifeless Manny on the shore before Sarah, his father, police, and everyone else, and must finally speak his truth aloud—confessing they built everything together and that Manny 'really loved you'—this ultimate exposure resolves the main story question of whether Hank can accept himself, demonstrating his arc completion as he owns his creation despite mortification.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND BEACH #0 - DAY 1
scenes.summary: Hank stands on a cooler with a noose around his neck, attempting suicide, but hesitates repeatedly until he spots a body floating in the waves.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hank's suicide attempt is interrupted by the appearance of the corpse, setting the entire story in motion

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND BEACH #0 - MOMENTS LATER 2
scenes.summary: Hank attempts to hang himself again with a belt, but the body on the beach begins farting repeatedly, distracting him from his suicide attempt.

scenes.heading: EXT. ISLAND OCEAN - CONTINUOUS 3
scenes.summary: Hank rides the farting corpse like a jet ski across the ocean by singing and pulling on the man's tie, escaping the island in a moment of rapturous freedom.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hank discovers the corpse's supernatural propulsion ability and uses it to escape the island

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH #1 - MORNING 5
scenes.summary: Hank wakes on a beach believing the jet ski escape was imagined, but discovers a bag of intact cheese puffs floating in the waves.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH #1 - LATER 6
scenes.summary: Hank sits across from the propped-up corpse, debates his sanity, then walks away but stops and looks back.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH #1 - LATER 7
scenes.summary: Hank drags the body down the beach by its armpits while yelling for help, then tries dragging it by the ankles instead.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH #2 - LATER 8
scenes.summary: Hank tries multiple methods to drag the body across the beach, eventually rolling it like a rug, then gets an idea.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH #2 - LATER 12
scenes.summary: Hank marches up a sandy hillside with the corpse strapped to his back, heading toward the forest.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #1 - CONTINUOUS 13
scenes.summary: Hank pushes through the forest with the corpse on his shoulder, attempts awkward conversation, and the body farts repeatedly as they bump into obstacles.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #2 - AFTERNOON 14
scenes.summary: Hank hikes through the forest while the corpse continues farting, then throws it down in frustration after spotting trash including a champagne cork.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #3 - MOMENTS LATER 15
scenes.summary: Hank walks through the forest with the corpse no longer farting, and begins singing to fill the silence.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #4 - TWILIGHT 16
scenes.summary: Hank realizes he's humming Cotton-Eyed Joe, comments on it to the corpse, then frantically tries to start a fire by rubbing sticks.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #4 - NIGHT 17
scenes.summary: Hank lies awake in the dark without fire, frightened by forest sounds, and places the corpse's arm over himself for comfort.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #4 - MORNING 18
scenes.summary: Hank wakes to find the corpse's arm moving, discovers a raccoon gnawing on it, chases the raccoon away, and smells the corpse's terrible odor.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #4 - LATER 19
scenes.summary: Hank disguises himself in ferns to ambush the raccoon but misses and hits the corpse instead, then storms off leaving it behind.

scenes.heading: EXT. MISC FOREST - LATER 20
scenes.summary: Hank marches through the forest alone, moving faster but looking guilty, continuing to yell for help.

scenes.heading: EXT. MISC FOREST - LATER 21
scenes.summary: Hank finds trash arranged like a body, discovers a Sports Illustrated Swimsuit Edition in a plastic bag, and slips it into his pants.

scenes.heading: EXT. MISC FOREST - LATER 22
scenes.summary: Hank eats red berries from a bush, then violently vomits behind a tree moments later.

scenes.heading: EXT. MISC FOREST - LATER 23
scenes.summary: Hank walks aimlessly checking his phone for service as the battery drops to 9%, realizes he's walked in a circle back to the corpse, and throws trash at it.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAVE - EVENING 25
scenes.summary: Hank inspects his rain catcher in the pouring rain, takes a small sip of collected water, and shares the last bit with the corpse.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE - NIGHT 26
scenes.summary: Hank lies awake shivering in the cave, hums mournfully, uses the corpse's jacket as a pillow, sings a song his mother sang, and calls himself crazy.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE - MORNING 27
scenes.summary: Hank wakes to rustling, sees the raccoon behind the corpse, tells it he wants to eat it, then lunges and chases it outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAVE - CONTINUOUS 28
scenes.summary: Hank throws rocks at the fleeing raccoon, accidentally hits and spills his rain catcher, frantically tries to save the water, then gives up defeated.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE - CONTINUOUS 29
scenes.summary: Hank considers abandoning the corpse but notices water dribbling from its mouth, pushes on its belly to spray water, fills a bottle, drinks it, and the corpse begins talking and asking questions.
scenes.turningPointNote: The corpse comes to life as Manny, transforming from object to companion and fundamentally changing the story's dynamic

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE (MONTAGE) 30
scenes.summary: Hank shows Manny various trash items trying to jog his memory and creates garbage figurines to teach him about different types of people and life.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAVE (MONTAGE) 30A
scenes.summary: Hank physically teaches Manny about blinking, sneezing, body parts, and facial expressions, then explains poop when Manny asks about animal droppings.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE (MONTAGE) 30B
scenes.summary: Hank uses garbage figurines to teach Manny about death in various forms, explains that dead people are hidden like trash, and tells Manny he's different because he talks and has magic.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAVE - CONTINUOUS 34
scenes.summary: Hank paces looking for direction while Manny stares at the Sports Illustrated; Hank explains sex and imagination, shares a memory about finding such magazines as a kid, and Manny's erection points a direction.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manny's erection becomes a compass pointing home, establishing their navigation method and deepening the absurdist logic

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #5 RIDGE - DAY 37
scenes.summary: Hank hikes with Manny strapped to his back while Manny asks if thinking about sex moves Hank's penis and requests to see it.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #6 - DAY 38
scenes.summary: Hank holds the magazine out for Manny but it's not working; Manny doesn't understand what to do with body parts, so Hank reluctantly whispers instructions in his ear.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #7 - AFTERNOON 39
scenes.summary: Hank whispers to Manny but nothing happens; Hank explains diminishing returns and reluctantly begins explaining fetishes to Manny.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #8 - AFTERNOON 40
scenes.summary: Hank drags Manny through the forest while Manny asks about sex and masturbation; Hank reveals his father caught him with a magazine and shamed him, making him feel disgusting.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #8 - AFTERNOON 41
scenes.summary: Hank apologizes for yelling and asks Manny if they're going the right way.

scenes.heading: EXT. DENSE FOREST #9 - SUNSET 42
scenes.summary: Hank gets tangled in thorny vines, steps in enormous animal droppings, falls, and his phone turns on in his pocket draining the battery to 8% as something rustles in the bushes.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAVINE - LATE AFTERNOON 43
scenes.summary: Hank lands painfully in a ravine, sees Manny staring at the girl on his phone with the battery at 6%, notices Manny's erection pointing up the opposite cliff face.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAVINE - EVENING 44
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny attempt to climb the cliff but fall; Hank finds a box of intact unpopped popcorn and eats a raw kernel in desperation.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAVINE - LATER 45
scenes.summary: Hank dresses in a garbage dress and red wig to impersonate the girl from the phone; Manny calls him beautiful but can't remember anything, then desperately pleads for another chance.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAVINE - NIGHT (MONTAGE) 46
scenes.summary: Hank breaks sticks, uses Manny's teeth as a knife and head as a hammer, and pulls scrap metal and wooden pallets from the dirt.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAVINE - BUS SET - DAWN 47
scenes.summary: Hank builds a bus set recreation of the phone photo and coaches Manny on how to talk to the girl, teaching him about conversation, kissing, and appropriate timing while role-playing as Sarah.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE - (DREAM SEQUENCE) 48
scenes.summary: Images flash through Manny's brain showing the name Sarah and contact information for Sarah Johnson.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAVINE - BUS SET - DAWN (BACK TO PRESENT) 49
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny realize her real name is Sarah Johnson; Manny's face twitches and smiles on its own, and he declares they must find Sarah so he can have twins with her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manny remembers Sarah's name and gains facial movement, establishing a concrete goal and demonstrating his increasing humanity

scenes.heading: EXT. RAVINE - DAY (MONTAGE) 50
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny celebrate by building a fire, popping all the popcorn, eating and dancing around the flames, then begin building a movie theater set.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAVINE - MOVIE THEATER SET - NIGHT (MONTAGE) 51
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny watch homemade shadow puppet movies together; Manny chokes on popcorn and when Hank performs the Heimlich, the kernel explodes a dummy's head.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAVINE - MORNING (MONTAGE) 52
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny practice shooting objects from Manny's mouth, load a grappling hook, fire it up and out of the ravine, and climb out together.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST - DAY (MONTAGE) 53
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny hike through the forest hunting; they shoot and kill a raccoon and a crow using Manny's mouth gun.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - DAY (MONTAGE) 54
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny discover a river, machine-gun the fish, celebrate with a bro hug, and Hank bathes in water Manny barfs while hanging upside down.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - EVENING (MONTAGE) 55
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny sit beside a fire roasting fresh animals.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - DINER SET - SUNSET (MONTAGE) 56
scenes.summary: Hank dressed as Sarah and Manny eat a feast together at a diner set, sharing raccoon wings, burgers, and fish meat sundaes.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - LIVING ROOM SET - NIGHT (MONTAGE) 58
scenes.summary: Hank puppets Manny to dance by firelight at a living room set; they dance together until Manny's arm swings violently forward karate chopping the mantle, then high-five with intense force.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAR SET - DAY (MONTAGE) 59
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny practice karate moves, convert a burnt golf cart into a car set using Manny as a jack, then sit in it as Hank dressed as Sarah smiles while they pass miniature road trip scenery.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - EVENING (MONTAGE) 60
scenes.summary: Hank uses Manny's strength to lift a log and finds a half-empty vodka bottle, which he feeds to Manny.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - PARTY SET - NIGHT (MONTAGE) 61
scenes.summary: Dummies fall from trees to create an elaborate Halloween party set where Manny opens beer bottles with his teeth and he and Sarah/Hank dance, drink vodka, and sing karaoke.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - ROOFTOP - NIGHT 62
scenes.summary: Drunk on the rooftop, Manny asks Sarah/Hank to make a ring with his fingers and proposes marriage, but Hank pulls back from their near-kiss, citing his parents' unhappy marriage.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manny proposes to Sarah/Hank and they nearly kiss, but Hank pulls away at the last moment, creating emotional distance and unresolved tension between them.

scenes.heading: EXT. MISC FOREST - DAY (FLASHBACKS) 63
scenes.summary: A montage of flashback moments shows Hank dancing, eating, holding hands, singing, and experiencing joy throughout their journey together.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - ROOFTOP SET - NIGHT (BACK TO PRESENT) 64
scenes.summary: Sarah/Hank lies in Manny's lap and they discuss what would make Hank happy; they lean in to kiss but Hank pulls away, blaming drunkenness, and they fall asleep together.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - ROOFTOP SET - MORNING 65
scenes.summary: Hank wakes awkwardly in Manny's lap, avoids eye contact, and silently ties Manny to his back to continue their journey.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER BEND - DAY 66
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny reach a cliff above a river bend and must decide whether to descend a steep wall or cross an old rusty utility pipe.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER BEND - LATER 67
scenes.summary: Hank powers on the cell phone and they watch it search for signal; when Sarah's photo appears with the boner pointing across the river, Hank powers the phone off without responding to Manny's gratitude.

scenes.heading: EXT. PIPE - DAY 68
scenes.summary: While crawling across the pipe, Manny describes feeling something stuck between them and asks if it's a thing; Hank cruelly dismisses him, making Manny feel worse.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - CONTINUOUS 69
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny fall through the air and slam into the water.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER UNDERWATER - CONTINUOUS 70
scenes.summary: Hank coughs out all his air as they rush underwater.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - DINER SET - NIGHT (DREAM SEQUENCE) 71
scenes.summary: A flash shows Sarah/Hank and Manny eating burgers in the diner set with water pouring over everything.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAR SET - DAY (DREAM SEQUENCE) 72
scenes.summary: A flash shows Sarah/Hank and Manny in their crashed car with water pouring over everything.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER UNDERWATER - (BACK TO PRESENT) 73
scenes.summary: Underwater, Hank is yanked backward by the rope as Manny sinks like a cinder block; Hank swims to Manny and wraps his arm around him, their eyes locking.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAVINE - BUS SET - DAY (DREAM SEQUENCE) 74
scenes.summary: A flash shows Sarah/Hank and Manny holding hands underwater on the bus set.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - PARTY SET - NIGHT (DREAM SEQUENCE) 75
scenes.summary: A flash shows Sarah/Hank and Manny dancing underwater by the fire.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - ROOFTOP SET - NIGHT (DREAM SEQUENCE) 76
scenes.summary: A flash shows Sarah/Hank and Manny almost kissing underwater on the rooftop.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER UNDERWATER - (BACK TO PRESENT) 77
scenes.summary: Shocked by visions and losing oxygen, Hank impulsively kisses Manny and uses him as an air tank, inhaling deeply while bubbles erupt from their lips in a romantic slow-motion moment.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hank kisses Manny underwater to use him as an air tank, discovering a new power while creating an intimate moment that blurs the line between survival and connection.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - MOMENTS LATER 78
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny erupt from the water like Free Willy in slow motion, float through the air singing, crash back into the water, crawl onto shore laughing, and do their secret handshake.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #10 - EVENING 79
scenes.summary: Hank folds Manny over in an intimate dance to create a spark and fireball, then happily eats fire-roasted squirrel by the fire.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #10 CAMPFIRE - NIGHT 80
scenes.summary: By the campfire, Hank tells Manny about his estranged relationship with his father and how they only communicate through automated birthday e-cards, revealing he could have died and his father wouldn't know.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #11 - MOMENTS LATER 81
scenes.summary: While peeing alone in the woods, Hank sees a flash of light and discovers a two-lane road about 50 feet away; he pulls out the cell phone and starts powering it on.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hank discovers they are near civilization and a road, marking the imminent end of their journey and forcing him to confront the truth about Sarah.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #10 CAMPFIRE - CONTINUOUS 82
scenes.summary: Manny sits alone at the campfire and begins softly humming to himself.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #11 - CONTINUOUS 83
scenes.summary: Hank gets signal on the phone and checks 21 unread messages and voicemails, finding only automated messages, expired paychecks, spam, and LinkedIn invites; he hovers over calling his dad but doesn't.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #10 CAMPFIRE - LATER 84
scenes.summary: Hank returns to tell Manny the truth about the phone being his, not Manny's, but freezes when Manny points out a giant raccoon eating their food; Hank drops the phone.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hank attempts to confess the truth about Sarah to Manny but is interrupted by a bear, and drops the phone, setting up the confrontation and danger to come.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE - (DREAM SEQUENCE) 85
scenes.summary: A flash shows Manny in a cave receding into darkness.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #10 CAMPFIRE - (BACK TO PRESENT) 85A
scenes.summary: Hank tries to shoot rocks from Manny's mouth at the bear but they fall out weakly; he slaps the bear and tries to run but falls with Manny on top of him; the bear slashes Hank's leg open and Manny realizes Sarah wasn't his.
scenes.turningPointNote: The bear attacks and wounds Hank while Manny discovers the truth about Sarah not being his, triggering both physical danger and emotional betrayal simultaneously.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST SETS (DREAM SEQUENCE) 87
scenes.summary: Flashes show Hank and Manny crashing through their memories with Sarah as uncontrollable thoughts race through Manny's mind.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREE TOPS - (BACK TO PRESENT) 88
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny float above the treetops, see the town lights all around them, then crash back into the trees and get tangled in branches; Hank sees headlights on the nearby road and tries to call for help.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE - DAY (DREAM SEQUENCE) 89
scenes.summary: A flash shows Manny at a funeral set saying goodbye to the phone in a casket, touching Sarah's face on the screen.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREE TOPS - (BACK TO PRESENT) 90
scenes.summary: Hank discovers Manny is crying for the first time; Manny convulses and doesn't like the wet, uncomfortable feeling while Hank's blood soaks through his pants.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE - DAY (DREAM SEQUENCE) 91
scenes.summary: Flashes of random thoughts—Cheetos, Sports Illustrated, fist bumps, Terminator 2—assault Hank's mind.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREE TOPS - (BACK TO PRESENT) 92
scenes.summary: Hank collapses on the tree branch from the mental deluge and begs Manny to stop thinking, but Manny says he can't help it.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST SETS (FLASHBACKS) 93
scenes.summary: Flashes show frozen moments from Manny's love story with Sarah—combing hair, holding hands, kissing, laughing—as if they are giant dioramas; the diner set tips and silverware falls.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREE TOPS - (BACK TO PRESENT) 94
scenes.summary: The diner set reveals they are actually high up in the trees; Hank grabs Manny's hand.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAVINE - BUS SET (DREAM SEQUENCE) 95
scenes.summary: A flash yanks Hank into the bus set where he and Manny hold hands and smile before headlights appear and an invisible car smashes the whole set.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREE TOPS - (BACK TO PRESENT) 96
scenes.summary: Hank is back on the tree branch, out of breath and sweating, and begins backing away from Manny in terror.

scenes.heading: EXT. MISC FOREST - (FLASHBACKS) 97
scenes.summary: Flashes show Hank secretly farting in various locations, hiding it from Manny.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - PARTY SET - NIGHT (DREAM SEQUENCE) 98
scenes.summary: A flash shows them in the party set with dummies around them, hearing the bear take down dummies one by one as Manny questions why they have to hide everything.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAVINE - THEATER SET (DREAM SEQUENCE) 99
scenes.summary: A flash shows Hank and Manny in the theater watching themselves on the tree branch.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE - DAY - (DREAM SEQUENCE) 100
scenes.summary: Flashes show Manny being buried in trash and a finger pushing his boner inward until it fully inverts.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAR SET - DAY (DREAM SEQUENCE) 101
scenes.summary: A flash shows Manny looking through the car window, watching Hank make out with himself dressed as Sarah.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREE TOPS - (BACK TO PRESENT) 102
scenes.summary: Hank tips over in a daze and his hands begin to slide off the branch.

scenes.heading: INT. CAVE - DAY (DREAM SEQUENCE) 103
scenes.summary: A flash shows a giant cork slamming into Hank's mouth, silencing him, and he spins and falls into the black void, grasping a tree branch.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREE TOPS - (BACK TO PRESENT) 104
scenes.summary: Hank dangles from the branch as Manny questions his weird brain and what to do with these thoughts; Hank's legs dangle and blood drips from his shoe.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - ROOFTOP SET - NIGHT (DREAM SEQUENCE) 105
scenes.summary: A flash shows Hank placing a rock in Manny's mouth on the rooftop; as they're about to kiss, Hank pushes Manny's stomach and the rock shoots through Hank's head like a shotgun.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - PARTY SET - NIGHT (DREAM SEQUENCE) 106
scenes.summary: A flash shows Hank falling backward in the party and bumping into a hanging dummy, which he turns around to reveal is himself hanging from a noose.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREE TOPS - (BACK TO PRESENT) 107
scenes.summary: Hank tries desperately to pull himself up as Manny says he wishes he was dead again; Hank tells Manny he understands, then his hand slips and he falls off the branch.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manny expresses suicidal thoughts and Hank, recognizing them from his own experience, admits he understands before falling from the tree—a moment of shared despair and physical crisis.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREE TOPS (DREAM SEQUENCE) 107A
scenes.summary: Hank falls past branches in slow motion through a contrived rig like the bus set; his ankle makes contact with the ground and twists to the side.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #10 CAMPFIRE - CONTINUOUS 108
scenes.summary: Hank grabs his twisted and bitten ankle in agony; he holds his breath and plays dead as the bear bites his ankle and drags him away while he looks up at Manny helplessly and tells him it's not his fault.
scenes.turningPointNote: The bear drags Hank away to his apparent death while he absolves Manny of guilt and admits he would have killed himself eventually anyway—a moment of acceptance and potential finality.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREE TOPS - CONTINUOUS 109
scenes.summary: Manny, stuck in a tree looking at bird poop, laughs and tells Hank that his brain may have invented Manny to distract from the inevitability of death and bodily decay.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #10 CAMPFIRE - (BACK TO PRESENT) 111
scenes.summary: As Hank is dragged by the bear, he and Manny continue their conversation about death, with Manny describing how organs and cells will decompose until nothing remains.

scenes.heading: EXT. MISC FOREST - DAY (DREAM SEQUENCE) 112
scenes.summary: A dream sequence shows animal poop scattered in the forest, trees falling, trash tumbling, and Manny's face rotting and melting.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #10 CAMPFIRE - (BACK TO PRESENT) 113
scenes.summary: Hank, still being dragged by the bear with tears in his eyes, smiles and tells Manny that everyone's decay mixing together sounds nice because their remains might meet someday, then peacefully closes his eyes.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREE TOPS - CONTINUOUS 114
scenes.summary: Manny, touched by Hank's words, calls him disgusting and lies motionless with a slight smile before suddenly twitching.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #10 CAMPFIRE - (BACK TO PRESENT) 116
scenes.summary: Hank notices movement in the treetops and looks up at Manny's silhouette.

scenes.heading: EXT. TREE TOPS - CONTINUOUS 117
scenes.summary: Manny twitches again, lies still, then shivers, shakes, and spasms as music swells.
scenes.turningPointNote: Manny reanimates, gaining mobility and agency for the first time, fundamentally changing the dynamic between the two characters.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #10 CAMPFIRE - CONTINUOUS 118
scenes.summary: Manny crashes to the ground, moves his limbs like a baby learning to walk, grabs a tree to stand but falls on his face, picks up Hank's sunglasses and puts them on, then starts crawling toward Hank while singing.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #10 CAMPFIRE - (BACK TO PRESENT) 120
scenes.summary: Hank rolls Manny around to extinguish flames on him, calls him an idiot affectionately, and Manny cradles Hank, puts sunglasses on him, and squeezes water from his belly into Hank's mouth before Hank passes out.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD - AFTERNOON 121
scenes.summary: Hank wakes up riding on walking Manny's back with his ankle bandaged, and they approach a house where Sarah is doing dishes; Hank panics and struggles to untie himself as Manny marches toward the house singing.
scenes.turningPointNote: They reach civilization and Sarah's actual house, forcing the fantasy to collide with reality and triggering Hank's panic about exposure.

scenes.heading: INT. SARAH'S HOUSE - CONTINUOUS 122
scenes.summary: Sarah hums while doing dishes, completely unaware that Hank and Manny are rolling around fighting in her yard outside the window.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD - CONTINUOUS 123
scenes.summary: Manny pushes on Hank's belly and chest, asking why Hank never farts or cries like he makes Manny do; Hank apologizes for being selfish, and Manny tells him everyone is ugly and dying but one person being okay with that could make everyone feel less alone; a three-year-old girl named Crissie appears and asks if they're dead.
scenes.turningPointNote: Their fantasy world is shattered by discovery; Crissie's appearance forces the collision of Hank's private delusion with the real world and witnesses.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST SETS - (FLASHBACKS) 124
scenes.summary: Manny's love affair with Sarah plays backwards in flashback.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAVE - NIGHT (DREAM SEQUENCE) 125
scenes.summary: A dream sequence shows Manny in expanding blackness, life fading from his eyes, his jaw falling slack, fingers twitching one last time, then blackness consuming him completely as he becomes dead again.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD - (BACK TO PRESENT) 126
scenes.summary: Hank cries on Sarah's shoulder while she thinks he's happy to be home, unaware that Manny just died again or was ever alive or loved her.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD - LATE AFTERNOON 127
scenes.summary: Flashing lights and police tape appear on a suburban street as Hank is escorted to an ambulance, examined by a paramedic who gives him pain medication and a temporary cast, then sits eating a Big Mac while ignoring the chaos.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD - MOMENTS LATER 128
scenes.summary: Hank watches as people put Manny into a body bag; a coroner explains Manny appears to be an unclaimed bridge jumper whose body will sit in the morgue then receive a county-funded funeral no one attends; when Hank tries to speak up, his father appears and Hank flees with Manny's body.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hank's father's appearance triggers Hank to steal Manny's body and flee, rejecting rescue and choosing Manny over returning to his old life.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOREST #10 - LATE AFTERNOON 129
scenes.summary: Hank drags Manny on a gurney pad through the forest, falls, and Manny slides down a hill like a sled; police pursue them as Hank rolls Manny onto the pad and they careen down toward the river.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - CONTINUOUS 130
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny crash into the river and float downstream on cushions while police hike alongside; Hank apologizes to Manny and tries to wake him, then spots a police officer on the pipe ahead and swims toward shore.

scenes.heading: EXT. PIPE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The officer climbs off the pipe into the woods.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - DINER SET - CONTINUOUS 133
scenes.summary: Officers hike toward the river and discover the dining room set Hank built; Hank hides his face and swims toward the opposite shore, realizing everyone will see everything he created, and tells Manny not to worry about what they think.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAR SET - CONTINUOUS 134
scenes.summary: Sarah and Preston discover the car set that looks like Preston's actual car and matches Sarah's Instagram; Preston angrily calls for Crissie.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - LIVING ROOM SET - CONTINUOUS 135
scenes.summary: Crissie discovers props at the living room set and flips through the handmade copy of 'Everyone Poops' before her father grabs her.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - PARTY SET - CONTINUOUS 137
scenes.summary: Hank's Dad wanders through the party set and stands transfixed in front of dummies that look like himself and Hank's mom.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - CONTINUOUS 139
scenes.summary: Hank and Manny swim downstream together, running from everyone.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAVINE - BUS SET - CONTINUOUS 140
scenes.summary: Sarah pulls back branches to discover the bus set in the ravine, the bus she should take to work, and doesn't know how to react.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - PARTY SET - CONTINUOUS 141
scenes.summary: The Camera Man films the party set as he walks through it.

scenes.heading: EXT. SHORE - SUNSET 142
scenes.summary: Hank drags Manny onto a sand bar and tries to shove him into the ocean but he washes back; Hank cradles Manny looking for signs of life while Sarah stands twenty feet away watching, a police officer behind her, and Hank's Dad on the rocks; Hank apologizes to Manny for screwing him up and says he wanted to give him all the things Hank thought he didn't deserve, then turns to see Sarah approaching.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final confrontation: Hank's entire constructed world and his relationship with Manny are exposed to Sarah, his father, and authorities simultaneously, forcing total vulnerability.
